prompt: I'm pitching our new B2B analytics product to a VP of Marketing at a 500-person company next Tuesday. We've got three live pilot customers, two of them are well-known brands. The VP is skeptical of analytics tools because she's bought three in five years and none of them got used. I have 30 minutes. Help me prep.

assertions.text: Asks at least one clarifying question OR explicitly states an assumption about the audience/situation
Names a specific story arc or structure (e.g., Rags to Riches, No Easy Way, Man in a Hole, Pride & Fall) and explains why it fits
Addresses the VP's prior-bad-experience skepticism directly (does not paper over it)
Recommends using the pilot customers as social proof or named-brand evidence
Produces a structured artifact (outline, scene list, beat sequence) rather than a wall of prose
Ends with a revision handle: a question, an ask for input, or a 'here's what only you can fill in' callout
